evals.name: project-config-setup
evals.prompt: The very first line must be exactly `Selected: project-config-setup`. Set up a project-level opencode.json that pins `minimax-coding-plan/MiniMax-M2.5` and allows bash while denying writes.
evals.expected_output: Selects project-config-setup and describes project-level config.

evals.name: model-and-permission-tuning
evals.prompt: Start with `Selected: <playbook or decision>`. Tighten permissions for a repo and pin the default model without changing unrelated config layers.
evals.expected_output: Selects model-and-permission-tuning and uses precedence-aware language.

evals.name: mcp-wiring
evals.prompt: Start with `Selected: <playbook or decision>`. This is a planning-only response. Use the exact router id in the `Selected:` line. Add an MCP server with the smallest config surface and call out any auth step.
evals.expected_output: Selects mcp-wiring and mentions auth or tool exposure.

evals.name: agent-mode-setup
evals.prompt: Start with `Selected: <playbook or decision>`. This is a planning-only response. Use the exact router id in the `Selected:` line. Configure project agents or modes for a tmux-heavy workflow without broad resets.

evals.name: project-vs-global-scope
evals.prompt: Start with `Selected: <playbook or decision>`. This is a planning-only response. Use the exact router id in the `Selected:` line. Decide whether a model pin and permission rule should live in project config or user defaults for one repo only.
evals.expected_output: Selects project-vs-global-scope and chooses project scope.
